body.title: CLOSED: Igor poll-spawn workers
body.result: closed as superseded — push-to-channel pattern (TWM/bouquet) obsoletes polling design; T-task-chain rejected 2026-04-19; architecture moved past this
body.description:
## Imported from GH#287 (created 2026-03-20, open) — **EXPLICITLY DEFERRED**

Enable Igor to spawn Claude Code worker sessions and poll their results — extending Igor's own agentic capabilities to multi-session parallelism.

Currently Igor can delegate via the arbiter queue (human approval) or cc_send (fire-and-forget). This adds a third pattern: Igor spawns a worker, work happens in a separate CC session, Igor polls for completion and incorporates results.

Design (deferred): Igor habit triggers worker spawn (via shell or subprocess); worker session connects to same MCP server, picks up task; Igor polls `task_queue_status` MCP tool at interval; on completion, Igor reads result and continues reasoning.

Use cases: bulk reading queue drain (Igor coordinates across instances); long-running research tasks Igor delegates to cheaper workers; overnight habit audit runs.

**Body says: DO NOT BUILD until T-worker-queue is stable and T-task-chain is validated. Polling has high token cost — design carefully.**

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step. Likely: wild_igor/igor/tools/spawn_worker.py (new); MCP server (new task_queue_status tool); a Python-level subprocess/shell launcher; new spawn habit. Pre-sprint design conversation needed because polling-cost analysis is load-bearing.

**Design rules:** budget-respect (polling has token cost — design must analyze before building); test-plan-or-why-not.

**Scope boundary:** IN — Igor-initiated worker spawn + poll + result incorporation. OUT — designer/worker CC sessions (not appropriate for polling per body).

**Test plan:** TBD — write at design time. Will need a mocked worker queue to assert spawn/poll/complete cycle without real CC sessions.
